title: Protect your PC from unwanted software
license_note: Screenshots from support.microsoft.com. Confirm redistribution with your legal team before use in a shipped product.

sections.title: Reduce the chances of getting unwanted software

steps.text: Download apps only from trusted sources, such as Microsoft Store, and be thoughtful about which apps you install.
Make sure you're running a regularly updated antivirus product such as Microsoft Defender Antivirus, which is built into all modern versions of Windows.
Windows 11 includes Smart App Control which can help to protect your device from potentially unwanted apps.
Use a modern browser, such as the new Microsoft Edge . Microsoft Edge uses Microsoft Defender SmartScreen , which helps block potentially unwanted apps.
Keep your device up-to-date with the latest updates for Windows, your browser, and your apps.

sections.title: If you believe you already have unwanted software

steps.text: Uninstall software you don’t need using Settings > Apps > Apps & features ( Add or Remove Programs in older versions of Windows). Open Apps & features
In Windows Security , get the latest security intelligence and then run a full scan with Microsoft Defender Antivirus . Learn how
If the unwanted software persists, you can submit the files to Microsoft .
Perform an offline scan with Microsoft Defender Offline .

sections.title: Report a download you believe has been incorrectly flagged

steps.text: Want more options? Discover Community Explore subscription benefits, browse training courses, learn how to secure your device, and more.
